docs.title: Executive summary
docs.text:
cicd-pipeline-train-schedule-git is a node.js / javascript application. Based on static analysis of the codebase, it implements a JavaScript/Node application. This page summarizes what the repository does, why it exists, and its major components.

  • What it does: Implements a javascript/node application.
  • Why it exists: Inferred from repository contents and the README (see excerpt below). This is a derived statement — confirm with the maintainers.
  • Major components: 5 top-level component(s); see Code Structure.

docs.title: Business capabilities
docs.text: • Serve a Node.js/JavaScript application.

docs.title: Technology stack
docs.text: Layer Detected technologies Languages JSON, JavaScript, CSS, Markdown Runtime deps (npm) cookie-parser, debug, express, http-errors, jade, lowdb, morgan

docs.title: Major components
docs.text: Component (top-level dir) Files views 3 routes 2 public 2 data 1 bin 1

docs.title: cicd-pipeline-train-schedule-git
docs.text: This is a simple train schedule app written using nodejs. It is intended to be used as a sample application for a series of hands-on learning activities.

docs.title: Running the app
docs.text:
It is not necessary to run this app locally in order to complete the learning activities, but if you wish to do so you will need a local installation of npm. Begin by installing the npm dependencies with:

npm install

Then, you can run the app with:

npm start

Once it is running, you can access it in a browser at http://localhost:3000
